page: Amazon Web Services g6.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz across 96 physical cores. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For acceleration, the instance integrates eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 179 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided by 7520 GB of local NVMe SSDs. The network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the g6.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, Passmark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations. Ray tracing and LLM inference tasks, specifically prompt processing and text generation for small to medium models, also rank in the top tier. However, single-core CPU performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average category, indicating a tradeoff where workloads must be highly parallelized to leverage the hardware fully.

Given the density of eight dedicated GPUs and 192 vCPUs, this instance offers high resource density for parallel computing. It is suited for large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, graphics-intensive rendering, and database operations that require high-throughput local NVMe storage and high-speed networking.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs from AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance is equipped with eight NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs providing 179 GB of total VRAM, 7520 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In performance testing, the server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU benchmarks, ray tracing, and LLM inference for small and medium models, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware configuration is designed for parallelized workloads, including large-scale LLM inference, graphics rendering, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 7520 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for parallel computing and LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.48xlarge features 192 AMD vCPUs, eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 7.5 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A highly parallelized x86 server featuring eight Ada Lovelace GPUs, massive NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core and LLM inference performance.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.4 GHz
Eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 179 GB total VRAM
7520 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and multi-core benchmarks
Suited for parallelized graphics rendering, machine learning, and databases
